mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS increases by >0.25%, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0% threshold, labor market deterioration signals impending recessionary pressures that systematically reduce S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both grow >2% month-over-month, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals strengthening economic momentum that generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns, but only during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration predicts negative or flat 5-day returns during stable policy periods, or the predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS volatility exceeds 0.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth rates reflect robust aggregate demand that supports corporate profit expansion and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.5%, or the signal shows no statistical edge (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment indicates labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive 5-day returns, or the interaction effect reverses sign during periods of wage stagnation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth predicts positive quarterly S&P 500 returns only when short-term interest rates remain below 2.0%, as low borrowing costs amplify supply-side momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth predicts negative returns when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%, or the conditional predictive power disappears in walk-forward validation.
